title: Update Morty prompts for Opus 4.7 behavior changes (literal instructions, fewer emojis, fewer subagents)
source_context: Official Opus 4.7 migration guide at platform.claude.com/docs/en/about-claude/models/whats-new-claude-4-7 lists these as behavior changes requiring prompt updates. Our existing scaffolding ('double-check X', 'respond in N bullets') may now be either redundant or insufficient. [PRIORITY: Opus 4.7 migration wave — user-requested]
description: Opus 4.7 behavior changes that silently affect Morty output: (1) MORE LITERAL instruction following — will not generalize from one bullet to another; prompts must enumerate every case explicitly. (2) LESS verbose / response length calibrates to task complexity — Morty output will be terser unless asked for detail. (3) FEWER tool calls by default — Morty's may now skip Read/Grep when reasoning suffices; counter with explicit 'verify by reading the file' directive. (4) MORE DIRECT tone — less validation-forward; Rick persona is safer, but user-facing Morty output may feel blunter. (5) FEWER subagents spawned by default — our team-mode relies on subagent spawning; we must explicitly instruct team-lead to delegate. Update: delegate.py Morty system prompt templates (references/prompts.md, delegate.py:build_prompt).
proposed_changes: Audit all Morty identity/constraints in agents/*.json and references/prompts.md. Add explicit enumeration where bullets imply generalization. Add explicit 'read files before concluding' to counter fewer-tool-calls default. For team-lead agent prompts, add 'DELEGATE subtasks to teammates via send_team_message — do not attempt to do everything yourself' to counter fewer-subagents default.
